_source: SystemVerilog Assertions Handbook - FIFO Requirements & Verification Plan
_note: Extracted from industry-standard reference book, not synthetic data

functional_requirements.description: When push is active, data_in shall be stored into the FIFO buffer at the next clock cycle
functional_requirements.verification: Simulation - monitoring of properties and coverage

functional_requirements.description: When pop is active, data_out shall carry the data that was first stored into the FIFO but was not yet popped. Data_out shall be asserted in the same cycle of pop control
It is an error if a push with no pop control occurs on a full FIFO
It is an error if a pop control occurs on an empty FIFO
Data entered into the FIFO buffer shall be outputted in the same order that it is entered (first-in, first-out)
functional_requirements.verification: Verification of output sequence (i.e., first-in is first-out)

functional_requirements.description: When the FIFO reaches the maximum depth of the buffer (BIT_DEPTH), then the full flag shall be active
functional_requirements.sva_property: property p_fifo_full; @ (posedge clk) qFull |-> full; endproperty

functional_requirements.description: When the number of entries in the FIFO reaches or is greater than ¾ of the maximum depth (ALMOST_FULL), then the almost_full flag shall be active
functional_requirements.sva_property: property p_fifo_almost_full; @ (posedge clk) qAlmost_full |-> almost_full; endproperty

functional_requirements.description: When all the enqueued data has been dequeued, then the empty flag shall be active. A reset shall cause the empty flag to be active
functional_requirements.sva_property: property p_fifo_empty; @ (posedge clk) qEmpty |-> empty; endproperty

functional_requirements.description: When the number of entries in the FIFO reaches or is less than ¼ of the maximum depth (ALMOST_EMPTY), then the almost_empty flag shall be active
functional_requirements.sva_property: property p_fifo_almost_empty; @ (posedge clk) qAlmost_empty |-> almost_empty; endproperty

functional_requirements.description: The reset_n is an active low reset control that clears the pointers and the status flags. Reset shall result in: !almost_empty && !full && !almost_full && empty
The clk clock is the synchronous system clock for the FIFO for both read and write transactions, active on the positive edge. Clock shall be at 50% duty cycle
When either an overflow (push on full) or underflow (pop on empty) error has occurred, the error flag shall be asserted
functional_requirements.sva_property: property p_error_flag; @ (posedge clk) q_push_error or q_pop_error |=> error; endproperty

functional_requirements.description: The FIFO shall provide parameters: BIT_DEPTH (2**BIT_DEPTH = depth), WIDTH (data width), ALMOST_FULL (0.75 * depth), ALMOST_EMPTY (0.25 * depth)
The FIFO shall support a maximum rate of 25 MHz
The power shall be less than 0.01 watt at 25 MHz

test_strategies.name: Fixed Parameterization - Bit_depth
test_strategies.test_sequence: Configuration Setup: Buffer depth = 2**4, 2**5, 2**8; Width=16, 32; Pseudo-random push and pop transactions; Unique data patterns using random values
test_strategies.verification_criteria: Design compiles and elaborates correctly. Simulation - monitoring of properties and coverage. Verification of output sequence (i.e., first-in is first-out)

test_strategies.name: RESET - Reset applied when fill state of FIFO is at different levels
test_strategies.test_sequence: property p_t1_full; @ (posedge clk) full |=> !reset_n; property p_t2_afull; @ (posedge clk) almost_full |=> !reset_n; property p_t3_empty; @ (posedge clk) empty |=> !reset_n; property p_t4_aempty; @ (posedge clk) almost_empty |=> !reset_n
test_strategies.verification_criteria: Simulation - monitoring of properties and coverage

test_strategies.name: COVERAGE - Using the properties and sequences defined in 4.1
test_strategies.test_sequence: cover property (p_push_pop_sequencing); cover property (qFull); cover property (qEmpty); cover property (qAlmost_empty); cover property (qAlmost_full); cover property (qOffull); cover property (qOffEmpty); cover property (qOffAlmost_empty); cover property (qOffAlmost_full)
test_strategies.verification_criteria: Sequences must all have a coverage count of at least one

corner_cases.verification: Must trigger error flag and ap_push_error assertion
Must trigger error flag and ap_pop_error assertion

corner_cases.description: Reset when FIFO is almost full
Reset when FIFO is almost empty
Read and write pointer wraparound at boundaries

corner_cases.verification: Verify correct behavior with continuous operations

corner_cases.description: Various data patterns (all 0s, all 1s, random, walking patterns)

required_assertions.name: ap_push_error
required_assertions.description: Never a push and full and no pop
required_assertions.sva_code: sequence q_push_error; !(push && full && !pop); endsequence; ap_push_error : assert property (@(posedge clk) q_push_error);

required_assertions.name: ap_pop_error
required_assertions.sva_code: sequence q_pop_error; !(pop && empty && !push); endsequence; ap_pop_error : assert property (@ (posedge clk) q_pop_error);

required_assertions.name: ap_pop_data
required_assertions.description: Data out timing and data integrity
required_assertions.sva_code: property p_pop_data; @ (posedge clk) pop |-> data_out == data_fromQ; endproperty; ap_pop_data : assert property (p_pop_data);

required_assertions.name: ap_fifo_full
required_assertions.description: Full flag correctly reflects FIFO state when at maximum depth
required_assertions.sva_code: property p_fifo_full; @ (posedge clk) qFull |-> full; endproperty; ap_fifo_full : assert property (p_fifo_full);

required_assertions.name: ap_fifo_almost_full
required_assertions.description: Almost full flag at correct threshold (¾ depth)
required_assertions.sva_code: property p_fifo_almost_full; @ (posedge clk) qAlmost_full |-> almost_full; endproperty; ap_fifo_almost_full : assert property (p_fifo_almost_full);

required_assertions.name: ap_fifo_empty
required_assertions.description: Empty flag correctly reflects FIFO state when no data
required_assertions.sva_code: property p_fifo_empty; @ (posedge clk) qEmpty |-> empty; endproperty; ap_fifo_empty : assert property (p_fifo_empty);

required_assertions.name: ap_fifo_almost_empty
required_assertions.description: Almost empty flag at correct threshold (¼ depth)
required_assertions.sva_code: property p_fifo_almost_empty; @ (posedge clk) qAlmost_empty |-> almost_empty; endproperty; ap_fifo_almost_empty : assert property (p_fifo_almost_empty);

required_assertions.name: ap_fifo_ptrs_flags_at_reset
required_assertions.description: Reset brings FIFO to known state (empty, flags cleared)
required_assertions.sva_code: property p_fifo_ptrs_flags_at_reset; @ (posedge clk) !reset_n |-> ##[0:1] !almost_empty && !full && !almost_full && empty; endproperty; ap_fifo_ptrs_flags_at_reset : assert property (p_fifo_ptrs_flags_at_reset);

required_assertions.name: ap_error_flag
required_assertions.description: Error flag asserted on overflow or underflow
required_assertions.sva_code: property p_error_flag; @ (posedge clk) q_push_error or q_pop_error |=> error; endproperty; ap_error_flag : assert property (p_error_flag);

functional_coverage.description: All sequences must have coverage count of at least one
covergroups: Status flags coverage (full, empty, almost_full, almost_empty)
Operation types (push only, pop only, simultaneous, idle)

ALMOST_FULL.description: Threshold for almost full flag (¾ of depth)

ALMOST_EMPTY.description: Threshold for almost empty flag (¼ of depth)
